findings.title: XSS via innerHTML in dashboard (6 sites) — unresolved from prior review
findings.description: Six innerHTML assignments render unsanitized server-supplied strings directly into the DOM. Task titles, thread names, and agent output are user-controlled data that flow through the harness server and back to this dashboard. Any script tag or event-handler attribute in that data executes in the viewer's browser. This was flagged in both 2026-03-18 and 2026-03-18-2 reviews and remains unresolved.
findings.action: Replace innerHTML with textContent for plain-text fields. For fields needing HTML structure, construct nodes with createElement/appendChild. If innerHTML is unavoidable, apply DOMPurify.sanitize() before assignment.

findings.title: Path traversal in sandbox boundary (12 sites) — unresolved from prior review
findings.description: 12 path-handling sites in harness-sandbox accept caller-supplied path components without canonicalization or boundary verification. The sandbox crate is the security boundary that prevents spawned agents from escaping their project root. A path traversal defeats the entire containment model. This was flagged in prior reviews and remains unresolved.
findings.action: After constructing any path from user input, call std::fs::canonicalize() and assert the result starts_with(allowed_base_dir). Return a permission-denied error if the assertion fails. Add unit tests with '../' sequences and symlink targets confirming they are rejected.

findings.title: Path traversal in GC agent file operations (6 sites) — unresolved from prior review
findings.description: gc_agent.rs performs filesystem operations on paths derived from project_root and task metadata without canonicalization. The GC agent is privileged — it deletes worktrees and manages the draft store. A crafted project_root passed via the API could cause it to delete or read files outside any project boundary. Five additional sites in handlers/gc.rs exhibit the same pattern.
findings.action: Canonicalize all path arguments at the API boundary before passing them to GC logic. Verify the canonicalized path starts_with a known safe base directory. Reject requests with paths that escape the boundary.

findings.title: NEW: task_executor wraps review prompts with implement_from_prompt, overriding read-only constraints
findings.description: The prompts.rs fix (commit cd3d9cd) added 'DO NOT create PRs' constraints to periodic_review_prompt. However, task_executor.rs constructs the agent's actual prompt by calling implement_from_prompt(req.prompt, git) for ALL tasks that lack an issue or PR number — including periodic_review tasks. implement_from_prompt appends 'Create your PR targeting the {branch} branch on the {remote} remote' and 'On the last line print PR_URL=...', directly overriding the read-only constraints. The periodic_review_prompt content is wrapped in <external_data> as 'user-supplied content', making it lower priority than the harness instructions. This is why the review agent still creates PRs despite the constraint fix.
findings.action: In task_executor.rs, detect req.source == Some("periodic_review") BEFORE constructing first_prompt. For review tasks, use the prompt string directly (no implement_from_prompt wrapping): let first_prompt = req.prompt.as_deref().unwrap_or_default().to_string(). The is_review_task early-return added in a prior commit handles the output side; this fixes the input side.

findings.title: Nested lock acquisition — deadlock risk in project service (4 sites) — unresolved
findings.description: Four sites acquire a second lock while already holding a first. Under concurrent task execution, if any caller acquires the same locks in the opposite order a deadlock results. The project service is called from both HTTP handlers and background task runners, increasing lock inversion probability.
findings.action: Establish a strict global lock acquisition order documented in a comment at the top of AppState. Refactor the four nested-lock sites to acquire locks in canonical order, or structure so the outer guard is dropped before acquiring the inner lock.

findings.title: Nested lock acquisition — deadlock risk in task runner hot path — unresolved
findings.description: task_runner.rs holds a lock during task lifecycle transitions while attempting to acquire a second lock. The task runner is the hot path for all agent execution. A deadlock here blocks every in-flight task.
findings.action: Drop the outer RwLock guard before acquiring the inner one. Consider whether the Task*/Todo* consolidation (F008) would simplify the locking structure.

findings.title: TOCTOU races in DashMap access (exec.rs, workspace.rs, exec_policy.rs) — unresolved
findings.description: Four DashMap sites across handlers/exec.rs, workspace.rs, and harness-rules/exec_policy.rs use get() + insert() patterns instead of the atomic Entry API. Under concurrent HTTP requests for the same resource, two threads can both see 'not present' and the second write silently overwrites the first. In exec_policy.rs this could allow a policy to be applied twice or have its result clobbered.
findings.action: Replace all get() + insert() patterns with map.entry(key).or_insert_with(|| value) to make the check-and-insert atomic.

findings.title: Dual Task*/Todo* domain model across 7 files — unresolved
findings.description: Task* and Todo* types coexist in task_runner.rs, task_db.rs, task_queue.rs, intake/mod.rs, harness-core/error.rs, harness-core/agent.rs, and harness-rules tests. This makes it unclear which model is canonical and risks state divergence when only one model is updated.
findings.action: Audit all Todo* usage. If Todo* maps 1-to-1 to Task*, delete Todo* and update all callsites. If Todo* has a distinct semantic, document the difference explicitly. Either path should leave one authoritative domain model.

findings.title: 24 unwrap() calls in webhook handler — external traffic, high panic risk — unresolved
findings.description: webhook.rs has the highest unwrap() density in the main codebase (24 sites). Webhook handlers receive external HTTP traffic from GitHub, Feishu, and similar sources. Any malformed payload, unexpected field type, or missing header will panic the handler task.
findings.action: Replace each unwrap() with ? propagation or match/if-let. Return HTTP 400 for client-caused errors and HTTP 500 with tracing::error! for internal errors.

findings.title: 33 unwrap() calls in lang_detect — highest count, called per-task — unresolved
findings.description: lang_detect.rs has 33 unwrap() calls, the highest count in any single file. This module is invoked on every incoming task prompt. A regex compilation failure or unexpected input causes an immediate server panic.
findings.action: Compile all regex patterns once at startup using std::sync::OnceLock, so compilation failures surface at boot rather than at request time. Replace runtime parse unwrap() calls with proper error handling.

findings.title: 8 silent Result discards in WebSocket layer — unresolved
findings.description: websocket.rs silently swallows errors at 8 sites using .ok() or .unwrap_or_default(). WebSocket send errors (client disconnect, write back-pressure, frame encoding failure) are dropped with no trace, making connection issues invisible in production logs.
findings.action: Replace each .ok() with if let Err(e) = ... { tracing::warn!("ws: send error: {e}") }. Break or return on error to avoid writing to a closed connection.

findings.title: Hardcoded .db path constructions across 15+ files (80+ violations total) — unresolved
findings.description: task_db.rs, thread_db.rs, plan_db.rs, project_registry.rs, review_store.rs, router.rs, stdio.rs, scheduler.rs, http.rs, services/execution.rs, services/task.rs, services/project.rs, harness-core/db.rs, and harness-observe/event_store.rs each construct SQLite paths inline. Different entry points can construct different paths for conceptually the same database, fragmenting data. harness-core/src/config/dirs.rs already exists but is not consistently used.
findings.action: Implement or extend a single default_db_path(name: &str) function in harness-core::config::dirs. Update all DB-opening callsites to use it.

findings.title: Deduplication logic discards updated finding metadata — unresolved
findings.description: In persist_findings(), when a recurring finding is detected (same rule_id + file), only the review_id is updated. title, description, action, impact, priority, confidence, and effort fields are NOT refreshed. If finding severity or fix guidance changes between review runs, the stored record becomes stale.
findings.action: In the SQL UPDATE branch, update all mutable fields alongside review_id. Only the original insertion timestamp should be preserved.
